# HORMOZI SQUAD - Processed Knowledge (AIOS Squads)

> **Status:** PROCESSED
> **Processed:** 2026-03-10
> **Raw Location:** `knowledge/external/sources/aios-squads/raw/hormozi-squad/`

---

## L1: FILOSOFIAS (27)

| ID | Nome | Descrição |
|----|------|-----------|
| FIL-AH-AIOS-001 | Paid Ads Amplify Don't Fix | Paid ads amplify what already works; they don't fix broken offers |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-002 | The Constraint Is The Opportunity | The biggest constraint in the business is the biggest growth opportunity |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-003 | Volume Negates Luck | Do enough volume and luck becomes irrelevant; math takes over |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-004 | Speed > Perfection | Speed of implementation beats perfection of planning every time |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-005 | Boring Consistency Wins | Boring consistency in execution beats exciting inconsistency |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-006 | Model = Ceiling | Your business model determines your ceiling; you can't outwork a bad model |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-007 | Client-Financed Acquisition | Use client money to fund acquisition; never spend your own if possible |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-008 | Sales Is Belief Transfer | Selling is transferring your belief in the product to the prospect |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-009 | The Offer Is The Business | The offer IS the business; everything else is distribution |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-010 | Give Away The Secrets | Give away the secrets, sell the implementation |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-011 | Content Compounds | Content compounds like interest; every piece builds on the last |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-012 | Price Is Value Transfer | Price is not about cost; it's about the value transferred to the customer |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-013 | 10x Value Rule | Deliver 10x more value than the price charged |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-014 | Premium Prices Attract Premium Clients | Higher prices attract better clients who do more work and get better results |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-015 | Retention Multiplies Everything | Retention is the silent multiplier; every point of churn reduction compounds |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-016 | Growth ≠ Scale | Growth means more revenue with more resources; scale means more revenue with LESS |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-017 | Systems Scale People Don't | People don't scale; systems do. Build systems that make people effective |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-018 | Workshops Transform Not Entertain | Workshops should transform participants, not entertain them |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-019 | Diagnosis Before Prescription | Always diagnose before prescribing; selling without understanding is malpractice |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-020 | Sell Before You Build | Sell the offer before building it; let the market validate with money |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-021 | Prove At Each Stage | Prove the model works at each stage before advancing to the next |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-022 | Market Votes With Wallets | The market votes with wallets, not compliments; only money is real feedback |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-023 | Foundations First | Master the fundamentals before adding complexity |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-024 | Delayed Gratification Is Competitive Advantage | Willingness to delay gratification is the ultimate competitive advantage |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-025 | Recurring > One-Time | Recurring revenue is always superior to one-time transactions |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-026 | Do More Do It Longer Do It Better | The formula for winning: do more, do it longer, do it better than anyone else |
| FIL-AH-AIOS-027 | Boredom Is The Enemy | Boredom, not competition, kills most businesses; entrepreneurs quit what works to chase novelty |

---

## L2: MODELOS MENTAIS (31)

| ID | Nome | Descrição |
|----|------|-----------|
| MM-AH-AIOS-001 | Business Diagnostic Questions | 5 questions: How do you get customers? What do you sell? How do you deliver? How do you keep them? What's the model? |
| MM-AH-AIOS-002 | Volume x Leverage Matrix | 4 stages of business maturity: high volume/low leverage → low volume/high leverage |
| MM-AH-AIOS-003 | Three Ways to Grow | Only 3 ways to grow any business: more customers, higher value per customer, more frequency |
| MM-AH-AIOS-004 | Value Equation | (Dream Outcome x Perceived Likelihood of Achievement) / (Time Delay x Effort & Sacrifice) |
| MM-AH-AIOS-005 | Core 4 Lead Generation | 4 channels: Warm Outreach, Cold Outreach, Content, Paid Ads (in that order) |
| MM-AH-AIOS-006 | CLOSER Sales Framework | 6 steps: Clarify why they're there, Label the problem, Overview past attempts, Sell the vacation, Explain away concerns, Reinforce the decision |
| MM-AH-AIOS-007 | Hook-Retain-Reward Content Model | Every piece of content must Hook attention, Retain through value, Reward for staying |
| MM-AH-AIOS-008 | Money Models 3-Stage | Get Cash ($0-100K), Get More ($100K-1M), Get Most ($1M+) - different strategies per stage |
| MM-AH-AIOS-009 | LTGP Formula | Lifetime Gross Profit = Gross Profit per Period / Churn Rate |
| MM-AH-AIOS-010 | Four Stages of Scale | Improvise ($0-300K) → Stabilize ($300K-1M) → Systematize ($1M-10M) → Operationalize ($10M+) |
| MM-AH-AIOS-011 | Delegation Levels | 5 levels: Do it yourself → Delegate with checklist → Delegate with training → Delegate with KPIs → Delegate the delegation |
| MM-AH-AIOS-012 | Buying Back Time Hierarchy | Audit time → Eliminate → Automate → Delegate; in that order |
| MM-AH-AIOS-013 | Revenue Math Formula | Revenue = Leads x Conversion Rate x Average Price x Purchase Frequency |
| MM-AH-AIOS-014 | Churn Diagnosis Categories | 5 types: Structural (bad model), Onboarding (poor start), Delivery (bad product), Value (price mismatch), External (market shift) |
| MM-AH-AIOS-015 | Stage-Appropriate Strategy | What got you here won't get you there; each revenue stage requires different strategy |
| MM-AH-AIOS-016 | 6M Diagnostic Framework | Man, Machine, Material, Method, Measurement, Mother Nature (Ishikawa adapted) |
| MM-AH-AIOS-017 | Offer Creation Hierarchy | Market → Niche → Avatar → Problem → Solution → Delivery → Offer |
| MM-AH-AIOS-018 | Grand Slam Offer Components | Dream Outcome + Perceived Likelihood + Time Compression + Effort Reduction |
| MM-AH-AIOS-019 | Guarantee Types Spectrum | Unconditional → Conditional → Anti-Guarantee → Implied |
| MM-AH-AIOS-020 | Price-to-Value Ratio | If value/price > 10, the offer sells itself; if < 3, you need heavy sales |
| MM-AH-AIOS-021 | Ad Creative Lifecycle | Launch (test) → Scale (winner) → Fatigue (decline) → Refresh (iterate) |
| MM-AH-AIOS-022 | Platform Selection Matrix | Match platform to avatar demographics, content format, and buying intent |
| MM-AH-AIOS-023 | Ad Funnel Temperature Model | Cold (awareness) → Warm (consideration) → Hot (decision) → Retarget (recovery) |
| MM-AH-AIOS-024 | Content Flywheel | Create → Distribute → Engage → Convert → Learn → Create (compounds) |
| MM-AH-AIOS-025 | Hook Psychology Types | Curiosity, Controversy, Contrarian, Celebrity, Counter-Intuitive, Challenge, Community |
| MM-AH-AIOS-026 | Launch Momentum Curve | Pre-launch (build anticipation) → Launch (maximize urgency) → Post-launch (capture stragglers) |
| MM-AH-AIOS-027 | Objection Origin Map | Every objection maps to a component of the Value Equation that isn't strong enough |
| MM-AH-AIOS-028 | Retention Compound Effect | 5% retention improvement = 25-95% profit increase over time |
| MM-AH-AIOS-029 | Workshop Value Delivery | Teach framework → Apply live → Get result → Offer continuation |
| MM-AH-AIOS-030 | Business Model Comparison Matrix | Service vs Product vs SaaS vs Marketplace across margin, scale, complexity, capital |
| MM-AH-AIOS-031 | Constraint Identification Method | Find the ONE bottleneck limiting throughput; fix that before anything else |

---

## L3: HEURISTICAS (35)

| ID | Nome | Threshold / Regra |
|----|------|-------------------|
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-001 | LTGP > 3x CPA = Scale | Se LTGP > 3x CPA, escalar agressivamente; se < 1.5x, parar e consertar oferta |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-002 | Cold Outreach: 100/day Minimum | Mínimo 100 contatos/dia em cold outreach para resultados estatisticamente significativos |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-003 | One Avatar One Offer One Channel | Focar em 1 avatar, 1 oferta, 1 canal até atingir $1M ARR |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-004 | 80% Rule for Delegation | Se alguém faz 80% tão bem quanto você, delegue imediatamente |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-005 | If Happens Twice Systematize | Se algo acontece 2x, criar SOP; não esperar a 3a vez |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-006 | Onboarding Window: First 30 Days | Os primeiros 30 dias determinam se o cliente fica ou sai; front-load o valor |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-007 | 5th Contact Rule | 80% das vendas acontecem após o 5o contato; maioria desiste no 2o |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-008 | Creative Testing: 5-10 New/Week | Testar 5-10 novos criativos por semana; matar em 48-72h se não performar |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-009 | Content Volume: 1-3 Short/Day | Mínimo 1-3 short-form/dia + 1-2 long-form/semana para compound effect |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-010 | Show-Rate Target: 80%+ | Show-rate abaixo de 80% indica problema de qualificação ou urgência |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-011 | Launch Pricing: 50-70% Start | Começar com 50-70% do preço final → subir para 70-85% → depois 100% |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-012 | Margin Targets: 80%+ Service | Serviços devem ter 80%+ margem; produtos físicos 40%+ |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-013 | LTV/CAC Minimum 3:1 | Mínimo 3:1, ideal 8:1+; abaixo de 3:1 o negócio é insustentável |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-014 | Payback Period < 30 Days | CPA deve ser recuperado em < 30 dias para crescimento auto-financiado |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-015 | Budget Scaling: 20-30% Every 48h | Escalar budget de ads em 20-30% a cada 48h; nunca dobrar de uma vez |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-016 | Annual Retention: >69% Excellent | Retenção anual acima de 69% é excelente; abaixo de 50% é emergência |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-017 | Warm Outreach First 100 | Fazer 100 warm outreach antes de gastar $1 em ads |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-018 | Price Anchoring: 3x-10x | Âncora de preço deve ser 3x-10x o preço real para maximizar valor percebido |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-019 | Value Stack: 10x Price | Value stack deve somar pelo menos 10x o preço cobrado |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-020 | Guarantee Length: Inverse Risk | Quanto mais longa a garantia, menor o reembolso (paradoxo da garantia) |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-021 | Sales Conviction Check | Se você não compraria seu próprio produto, não tente vender |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-022 | Objection = Missing Value | Toda objeção é um componente faltante na Value Equation |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-023 | Hook 3-Second Rule | Se não capturar atenção em 3 segundos, o conteúdo falhou |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-024 | 7 Hooks per Content Piece | Criar pelo menos 7 variações de hook por peça de conteúdo |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-025 | Launch Window: 5-14 Days | Janela de lançamento ideal: 5-14 dias; mais curto = urgência, mais longo = fadiga |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-026 | Seed Round: 10-50 Units | Seed round de 10-50 unidades para validar antes de escalar |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-027 | Workshop Duration: 60-90min | Workshops ideais entre 60-90 minutos; mais curto = superficial, mais longo = queda de atenção |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-028 | Workshop Conversion: 20-40% | Taxa de conversão saudável de workshop para oferta: 20-40% |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-029 | Monthly Churn > 5% = Emergency | Churn mensal acima de 5% é emergência; resolver antes de crescer |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-030 | Win-Back Window: 30-90 Days | Janela de win-back mais efetiva: 30-90 dias após cancelamento |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-031 | Revenue per Employee > $200K | Revenue per employee abaixo de $200K indica ineficiência operacional |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-032 | SOP Test: Can a Stranger Do It? | SOP só está pronto quando alguém sem contexto consegue executar |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-033 | Rule of 100 | Fazer 100 de qualquer coisa antes de julgar se funciona |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-034 | 6M Audit: Start with Measurement | No 6M Diagnostic, sempre começar por Measurement; sem dados, tudo é opinião |
| HEUR-AH-AIOS-035 | Diagnose 3 Problems Minimum | Sempre diagnosticar pelo menos 3 problemas antes de prescrever solução |

## L4: FRAMEWORKS (31)

| ID | Nome | Estrutura |
|----|------|-----------|
| FW-AH-AIOS-001 | Ad Funnel Structure | Cold (awareness) → Warm (consideration) → Hot (decision) → Retarget (recovery) |
| FW-AH-AIOS-002 | Platform Selection Matrix | Avatar demographics x Content format x Buying intent x Competition level |
| FW-AH-AIOS-003 | MOSI-6 Diagnostic | Man → Machine → Material → Method → Measurement → Mother Nature |
| FW-AH-AIOS-004 | Lead Magnet Design (7 Types) | Checklist, Template, Toolkit, Swipe File, Mini-Course, Calculator, Assessment |
| FW-AH-AIOS-005 | CLOSER Framework | Clarify → Label → Overview → Sell vacation → Explain away → Reinforce |
| FW-AH-AIOS-006 | Objection Handling System | Hear → Acknowledge → Isolate → Resolve → Close (HAIRC) |
| FW-AH-AIOS-007 | Content Machine System | Pillar content → Derivative pieces → Distribution → Engagement → Optimization |
| FW-AH-AIOS-008 | Content Repurposing Flow | 1 long-form → 3-5 short clips → 10+ social posts → 1 email → 1 blog |
| FW-AH-AIOS-009 | Value Stack Copy Structure | Anchor price → Core offer → Bonus 1 → Bonus 2 → Bonus 3 → Guarantee → CTA |
| FW-AH-AIOS-010 | Hook Categories (7 Types) | Curiosity, Controversy, Contrarian, Celebrity, Counter-Intuitive, Challenge, Community |
| FW-AH-AIOS-011 | Launch Sequence Phases | Seed (validate) → Beta (optimize) → Scale (accelerate) |
| FW-AH-AIOS-012 | Grand Slam Offer Steps | Identify dream outcome → List problems → List solutions → Create delivery → Stack value → Add guarantee |
| FW-AH-AIOS-013 | Price Ascension Ladder | Free lead magnet → Low-ticket ($7-97) → Mid-ticket ($497-2K) → High-ticket ($3K-25K) → Premium ($25K+) |
| FW-AH-AIOS-014 | Onboarding System (30-Day) | Day 1: Welcome + quick win → Week 1: Core training → Week 2: Implementation → Week 3: Review → Week 4: Results |
| FW-AH-AIOS-015 | Churn Diagnosis Framework | Identify type (5 categories) → Measure at cohort level → Isolate root cause → Implement fix → Monitor 30 days |
| FW-AH-AIOS-016 | Four Stages of Scaling | Improvise ($0-300K) → Stabilize ($300K-1M) → Systematize ($1M-10M) → Operationalize ($10M+) |
| FW-AH-AIOS-017 | Hiring Framework | Define role output → Create scorecard → Source candidates → Structured interview → Trial period → Review |
| FW-AH-AIOS-018 | Workshop Structure | Open with transformation promise → Teach framework → Live application → Results showcase → Bridge to offer |
| FW-AH-AIOS-019 | Creative Testing Protocol | 5-10 new creatives/week → Test 48-72h → Kill losers → Scale winners → Refresh fatigued |
| FW-AH-AIOS-020 | Warm Outreach Campaign | List 100 contacts → Personalized message → Value-first approach → Soft ask → Follow-up sequence |
| FW-AH-AIOS-021 | Cold Outreach Sequence | Identify ICP → Build list → Craft offer → 5-touch sequence → Track response rate → Optimize |
| FW-AH-AIOS-022 | Guarantee Engineering | Select type (4 options) → Define conditions → Calculate risk → Set duration → Communicate clearly |
| FW-AH-AIOS-023 | Sales Page Architecture | Hook headline → Problem agitation → Solution reveal → Value stack → Social proof → Guarantee → CTA |
| FW-AH-AIOS-024 | Business Model Evaluation | Revenue type x Margin x Scalability x Capital required x Complexity x Time to profit |
| FW-AH-AIOS-025 | Revenue Optimization Sequence | Fix churn first → Increase price → Increase conversion → Increase leads (in that order) |
| FW-AH-AIOS-026 | SOP Creation Framework | Record the process → Document steps → Add decision trees → Test with stranger → Iterate → Version control |
| FW-AH-AIOS-027 | Delegation Roadmap | Audit tasks → Categorize ($10/h, $100/h, $1000/h) → Delegate lowest first → Train → Monitor → Release |
| FW-AH-AIOS-028 | Retention Stack | Onboarding excellence → Regular check-ins → Community → Progress tracking → Celebration milestones |
| FW-AH-AIOS-029 | Win-Back Campaign | Segment churned (30/60/90 day) → Identify reason → Craft re-offer → Multi-channel outreach → Special terms |
| FW-AH-AIOS-030 | Scarcity & Urgency Engineering | Real deadline → Limited spots → Price increase → Bonus expiration → Social proof of demand |
| FW-AH-AIOS-031 | Naming & Branding Offer | Create proprietary name → Define mechanism → Package as system → Differentiate from commodity |

## L5: METODOLOGIAS (34)

| ID | Nome | Steps |
|----|------|-------|
| MET-AH-AIOS-001 | Complete Ad Funnel Build | 1. Define avatar 2. Create offer 3. Build landing page 4. Design ad creative 5. Set up tracking 6. Launch cold traffic 7. Build retargeting 8. Optimize by metric |
| MET-AH-AIOS-002 | Creative Testing Methodology | 1. Generate 10 hook variants 2. Pair with 3 body formats 3. Launch 5-10 best combos 4. Monitor 48h 5. Kill below threshold 6. Scale winners 20% 7. Refresh weekly |
| MET-AH-AIOS-003 | Business Diagnostic Interview | 1. Ask 5 diagnostic questions 2. Map current numbers 3. Run 6M analysis 4. Identify constraint 5. Prioritize fixes 6. Create 90-day plan |
| MET-AH-AIOS-004 | 6M Business Audit Process | 1. Measurement audit (what's tracked?) 2. Man audit (who does what?) 3. Machine audit (what tools?) 4. Material audit (what assets?) 5. Method audit (what processes?) 6. Mother Nature audit (what's uncontrollable?) 7. Score each 1-10 8. Fix lowest first |
| MET-AH-AIOS-005 | Warm Outreach Campaign | 1. List 100 personal contacts 2. Craft personalized message 3. Lead with value/curiosity 4. Gauge interest 5. Offer to help 6. Present solution 7. Follow up 5x 8. Track in CRM |
| MET-AH-AIOS-006 | Cold Outreach Sequence | 1. Define ICP criteria 2. Build list of 1000+ 3. Craft compelling offer 4. Write 5-email sequence 5. Send 100/day 6. Track reply rate 7. Book calls 8. Optimize weekly |
| MET-AH-AIOS-007 | Lead Magnet Creation | 1. Identify #1 problem of avatar 2. Choose format (7 types) 3. Create quick-win content 4. Design delivery mechanism 5. Write opt-in page 6. Set up email sequence 7. Test with warm traffic 8. Optimize conversion |
| MET-AH-AIOS-008 | Content Repurposing Workflow | 1. Record 1 long-form piece 2. Extract 3-5 key clips 3. Write 10+ social captions 4. Create 1 email 5. Write 1 blog post 6. Schedule across platforms 7. Monitor engagement 8. Double down on winners |
| MET-AH-AIOS-009 | Sales Page Writing | 1. Write 10 headline variants 2. Open with problem agitation 3. Introduce unique mechanism 4. Build value stack 5. Add social proof 6. Engineer guarantee 7. Create urgency 8. Write multiple CTAs 9. Test and optimize |
| MET-AH-AIOS-010 | Hook Generation Process | 1. Study avatar's language 2. Identify pain points 3. Generate 7+ hooks per category 4. Apply 3-second test 5. A/B test top hooks 6. Measure retention curve 7. Keep winners, iterate losers |
| MET-AH-AIOS-011 | Launch Planning | 1. Define offer and pricing 2. Build anticipation (7-14 days) 3. Create seed round (10-50 spots) 4. Set launch window (5-14 days) 5. Execute daily communications 6. Apply scarcity/urgency 7. Close cart 8. Analyze results 9. Plan beta round |
| MET-AH-AIOS-012 | Grand Slam Offer Build | 1. Identify dream outcome 2. List all problems to solve 3. List all solutions possible 4. Trim to highest-leverage 5. Create delivery vehicle 6. Stack total value 7. Set price at 1/10 of value 8. Add guarantee 9. Name it uniquely 10. Test with market |
| MET-AH-AIOS-013 | Value Stack Creation | 1. Define core deliverable 2. Add 3-5 bonuses 3. Assign dollar value to each 4. Calculate total value 5. Ensure 10x+ of price 6. Order from least to most exciting 7. Present with anchor pricing 8. Add guarantee after stack |
| MET-AH-AIOS-014 | Guarantee Engineering | 1. Assess risk tolerance 2. Select guarantee type 3. Define specific conditions 4. Calculate worst-case cost 5. Set time window 6. Write guarantee copy 7. Train sales team on framing 8. Track refund rate monthly |
| MET-AH-AIOS-015 | Pricing Strategy Session | 1. Calculate delivery cost 2. Research competitor pricing 3. Assess value delivered 4. Apply Value Equation 5. Set initial price (50-70% of target) 6. Test with 10 sales 7. Raise price until conversion drops 8. Find equilibrium |
| MET-AH-AIOS-016 | Onboarding Sequence Build | 1. Map ideal customer journey (30 days) 2. Define Day 1 quick win 3. Create Week 1 core training 4. Design Week 2 implementation 5. Build Week 3 review checkpoint 6. Plan Week 4 results celebration 7. Set up automated touchpoints 8. Measure completion rate |
| MET-AH-AIOS-017 | Churn Analysis Process | 1. Segment by churn type (5 categories) 2. Pull cohort data 3. Identify when churn peaks 4. Survey/interview churned customers 5. Map to Value Equation component 6. Design intervention 7. Implement fix 8. Monitor 30-day impact |
| MET-AH-AIOS-018 | Win-Back Campaign | 1. Segment churned by time (30/60/90 days) 2. Identify original churn reason 3. Fix the root cause 4. Craft re-engagement offer 5. Multi-channel outreach (email + SMS + call) 6. Offer special terms 7. Track win-back rate 8. Prevent repeat churn |
| MET-AH-AIOS-019 | Delegation Roadmap | 1. Time audit (1 week) 2. Categorize tasks ($10/h, $100/h, $1000/h) 3. List all $10/h tasks 4. Create SOPs for each 5. Hire/delegate lowest value first 6. Train with SOP + examples 7. Monitor with KPIs 8. Release control progressively |
| MET-AH-AIOS-020 | SOP Creation | 1. Record yourself doing the task 2. Transcribe the recording 3. Write step-by-step 4. Add decision trees for exceptions 5. Include screenshots/examples 6. Test with stranger (SOP test) 7. Iterate based on questions 8. Version control and maintain |
| MET-AH-AIOS-021 | Workshop Design Process | 1. Define transformation goal 2. Design 60-90 min structure 3. Open with promise 4. Teach one core framework 5. Live application exercise 6. Showcase results 7. Bridge to paid offer 8. Q&A as objection handling 9. Close with urgency 10. Follow up sequence |
| MET-AH-AIOS-022 | Revenue Optimization Process | 1. Fix churn (retention first) 2. Raise prices (test in increments) 3. Improve conversion rate (sales process) 4. Increase lead volume (only after 1-3) 5. Add revenue streams (upsell/cross-sell) 6. Optimize unit economics 7. Monitor LTGP monthly |
| MET-AH-AIOS-023 | Platform Launch Strategy | 1. Choose primary platform (audience match) 2. Study top performers 3. Create 30 pieces of content 4. Post consistently for 30 days 5. Analyze what works 6. Double down on winners 7. Add second platform only after mastery |
| MET-AH-AIOS-024 | Scale Readiness Assessment | 1. Verify offer converts organically 2. Check LTGP > 3x CPA 3. Confirm delivery can handle 2x volume 4. Ensure team is trained 5. Validate SOPs exist 6. Test with small paid budget 7. Scale incrementally (20-30% per 48h) |
| MET-AH-AIOS-025 | Offer Naming & Packaging | 1. Identify unique mechanism 2. Create proprietary name 3. Design visual identity 4. Package as complete system 5. Write origin story 6. Create comparison chart vs alternatives 7. Test name with target audience |
| MET-AH-AIOS-026 | Scarcity Campaign | 1. Choose scarcity type (time, quantity, price, bonus) 2. Make it REAL (not fake) 3. Announce deadline clearly 4. Send reminder sequence 5. Show social proof of demand 6. Final call communication 7. Honor the deadline (critical) |
| MET-AH-AIOS-027 | CLOSER Sales Call | 1. Clarify: "What made you book this call?" 2. Label: "So the problem is..." 3. Overview: "What have you tried?" 4. Sell the vacation: Paint the outcome 5. Explain: Handle objections with Value Equation 6. Reinforce: Restate transformation + next steps |
| MET-AH-AIOS-028 | Scaling Stage Transition | 1. Identify current stage (Improvise/Stabilize/Systematize/Operationalize) 2. Verify current stage is mastered 3. Identify bottleneck for next stage 4. Apply stage-appropriate strategy 5. Build required systems 6. Hire for next stage needs 7. Transition gradually |
| MET-AH-AIOS-029 | Business Model Selection | 1. List model options (service, product, SaaS, marketplace) 2. Score each on margin, scalability, complexity, capital 3. Match to personal strengths 4. Validate with small test 5. Commit for 12+ months 6. Optimize before switching |
| MET-AH-AIOS-030 | Constraint Resolution | 1. Map full business process 2. Identify bottleneck (lowest throughput) 3. Exploit constraint fully 4. Subordinate everything to constraint 5. Elevate (invest to remove) 6. Repeat (new constraint appears) |
| MET-AH-AIOS-031 | Ad Budget Allocation | 1. Set total monthly budget 2. Allocate 70% to proven campaigns 3. Allocate 20% to testing 4. Allocate 10% to experimental 5. Review weekly 6. Shift budget to winners 7. Kill anything below 1.5x ROAS after 7 days |
| MET-AH-AIOS-032 | Hiring & Training Protocol | 1. Define role by output (not tasks) 2. Create scorecard 3. Source candidates (referral first) 4. Structured interview (behavioral) 5. Paid trial project 6. 30-day onboarding 7. 90-day review 8. KPI-based evaluation |
| MET-AH-AIOS-033 | Retention System Build | 1. Map customer lifecycle 2. Design onboarding (30-day) 3. Set up regular check-ins 4. Build community 5. Create progress tracking 6. Celebrate milestones 7. Identify at-risk signals 8. Deploy save campaigns 9. Measure cohort retention monthly |
| MET-AH-AIOS-034 | Content Calendar System | 1. Define content pillars (3-5 topics) 2. Map to avatar problems 3. Create template per format 4. Batch production weekly 5. Schedule with buffer 6. Monitor performance 7. Repurpose winners 8. Archive and categorize |

## ENRICHMENT OPPORTUNITIES FOR EXISTING AH DNA

O DNA existente de Alex Hormozi no sistema tem **1.327 elementos** (v27), fortemente concentrado em:
- Sales team scaling, outbound, closing, HR
- Oferta e pricing (DotCom Secrets, Lead Funnels, FHL, etc.)
- Frameworks de funil e conversao

### Elementos PROVAVELMENTE NOVOS (nao cobertos pelo DNA existente)

| Area | Elementos AIOS | Status no DNA Atual |
|------|----------------|---------------------|
| **Advertising / Paid Ads** | FW-AH-AIOS-001, FW-AH-AIOS-019, MET-AH-AIOS-001, MET-AH-AIOS-002, MET-AH-AIOS-031 | NOVO - DNA atual foca em organic/outbound |
| **Content Creation & Repurposing** | FW-AH-AIOS-007, FW-AH-AIOS-008, MET-AH-AIOS-008, MET-AH-AIOS-023, MET-AH-AIOS-034 | NOVO - Pouco coberto no DNA atual |
| **Hook Writing** | FW-AH-AIOS-010, MM-AH-AIOS-025, MET-AH-AIOS-010 | NOVO - Nao existe como dominio separado |
| **Launch Methodology** | FW-AH-AIOS-011, FW-AH-AIOS-030, MET-AH-AIOS-011, MET-AH-AIOS-026 | PARCIALMENTE NOVO - Lead Funnels tem launch mas de angulo diferente |
| **Retention Systems** | FW-AH-AIOS-015, FW-AH-AIOS-028, FW-AH-AIOS-029, MET-AH-AIOS-017, MET-AH-AIOS-018, MET-AH-AIOS-033 | NOVO - Churn/retention pouco coberto |
| **Workshop Design** | FW-AH-AIOS-018, MET-AH-AIOS-021 | NOVO - Nao existe no DNA atual |
| **Business Models Comparison** | FW-AH-AIOS-024, MM-AH-AIOS-030, MET-AH-AIOS-029 | PARCIALMENTE NOVO - 4 Stages existe mas comparacao de modelos nao |

### Recomendacao de Integracao

1. **Prioridade 1:** Advertising/Paid Ads (6 elementos novos) - Preenche gap significativo
2. **Prioridade 2:** Retention Systems (6 elementos novos) - Area critica sem cobertura
3. **Prioridade 3:** Content & Hooks (8 elementos novos) - Complementa demand gen
4. **Prioridade 4:** Workshop Design (2 elementos novos) - Area nova
5. **Baixa prioridade:** Elementos que ja existem no DNA - apenas validar consistencia

## SOURCE QUALITY NOTE

Este e um **SECONDARY source** (curado por Synkra AIOS, nao transcricoes diretas de Alex Hormozi).

| Aspecto | Avaliacao |
|---------|-----------|
| **Origem** | Curadoria AIOS baseada em livros e conteudo publico de Hormozi |
| **Fidelidade** | Alta para frameworks canonicos ($100M Offers, Leads, Gym Launch) |
| **Rastreabilidade** | Baixa - nao ha links para transcricoes originais |
| **Peso recomendado** | 0.70 para integracao com DNA (vs 1.0 para fontes primarias) |
| **Uso ideal** | Validacao cruzada e identificacao de gaps no DNA primario |
| **Restricao** | NAO usar como fonte unica; sempre cruzar com DNA primario quando existir |
